items.text:
Subject: Spring Career Workshop — Required Pre-Reading and Logistics

Dear Tomas,

This email confirms your registration for the Spring Career Workshop on Friday, March 13, in Whitfield Hall, Room 305, from 1:00 PM to 4:30 PM. Please read the items below carefully before you arrive.

- Submit your updated résumé through the careers portal no later than Wednesday, March 11 at 5:00 PM. Late uploads cannot be reviewed in time for the live session.
- Bring a printed copy of the case study attached to this message; the recruiter panel will reference it during the second hour.
- The $10 materials fee covers the workbook and snacks. Pay at the welcome table in cash; cards are not accepted on-site.
- A networking dinner follows the workshop at 5:00 PM in the Atrium. Attendance is optional but recommended for second-year students.

If you cannot attend, please email me by Tuesday, March 10, to release your spot for waitlisted classmates.

Best,
Renata Diaz
Career Services Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Spring Career Workshop — Required Pre-Reading and Logistics

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Tomas do before Wednesday, March 11?

options.A: Pay the $10 materials fee through the careers portal
options.B: Submit his updated résumé through the careers portal
options.C: Confirm his attendance at the networking dinner
options.D: Print extra copies of his résumé for the panel

questions.explanation: The email instructs Tomas to upload his updated résumé to the careers portal no later than Wednesday, March 11 at 5:00 PM, warning that late uploads cannot be reviewed in time.

questions.stem: What does the email indicate about the materials fee?

options.A: It is waived for second-year students at the dinner
options.B: It must be paid through the online careers portal
options.C: It is refundable if Tomas cancels by March 10
options.D: It must be paid in cash at the welcome table

questions.explanation: The email specifies that the $10 fee should be paid in cash at the welcome table because cards are not accepted on-site.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the case study mentioned in the email?

options.A: It will be discussed during the second hour of the workshop
options.B: It must be submitted through the careers portal beforehand
options.C: It will be handed out at the welcome table on Friday
options.D: It is optional reading for second-year students only

questions.explanation: The email says recruiters will reference the printed case study during the second hour, implying it becomes the focus of that portion of the workshop.
